headlineSummary: HN sentiment was mixed and close between Anthropic and OpenAI, with Anthropic slightly helped by business-value comments [E1][E2] while OpenAI drew both praise for Codex and criticism over profitability and creativity concerns [E1][E2][E3][E4][E6].

ranking.summary: Anthropic landed in a mixed-neutral pocket with a close tie to OpenAI; the evidence leans slightly positive on business value and possible profitability, though there is also a sharp complaint about Claude Code giving up on tasks [E1][E2][E4].

OpenAI was also mixed-neutral and close with Anthropic, combining strong praise for Codex features with negative comments about profitability and ChatGPT feeling creativity-draining [E1][E2][E3][E4][E6].
Microsoft Copilot had a mildly positive but limited-evidence showing, driven by one report that a Copilot-based workflow works quite well and is loved by the team [E5].

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic and OpenAI have raised (not spent yet, just raised) $317B. That's ~3% of knowledge worker spending in one year alone. What business wouldn't pay 3, 5 or 10% more a year to make their worker productivity increase by larger factors?

annotations.rationale: The commenter argues businesses would pay meaningful premiums for the productivity gains from these companies' AI, implying confidence in Anthropic's economic value proposition.
The commenter presents OpenAI's tools as valuable enough that businesses would rationally pay more for worker productivity gains.

evidence.excerpt: Rumor mill suggests that Anthropic might be profitable (but at what magnitude), OpenAI is not profitable

annotations.rationale: Saying Anthropic might be profitable is mild business praise, though hedged by uncertainty.
The commenter directly criticizes OpenAI's business position by stating it is not profitable.

evidence.excerpt: the Codex app's computer & browser use features have made this statement a lot weaker than it used to be. If you haven't tried them yet—they're mindblowing.

annotations.rationale: The speaker explicitly praises Codex app features as 'mindblowing,' which is strong positive sentiment toward OpenAI's product capabilities.

evidence.excerpt: I just want Claude Code to stop giving up on achieving tasks. It's so annoying. Even with `/goal` or the new `ultracode` it gives up constantly. My project is very complex (https://github.com/mohsen1/tsz) but Codex has no problem keep grinding without stopping like that

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly criticizes Claude Code for repeatedly giving up and calls that behavior annoying.
In direct contrast to Claude Code, Codex is described as able to keep grinding without stopping, which is mild praise.

evidence.excerpt: I built a more naive version for our team using Copilot and GitHub actions and it works quite well (wish I had metrics too). The team loves it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports good results from using Copilot for code review workflows and says their team loves it.

evidence.excerpt: Instead of Brainstorming something funny, we just prompted ChatGPT and chose one of the results. I felt lost immediately. All the creativity, the humanity, the endless hours of putting soul into something. Gone

annotations.rationale: The commenter describes ChatGPT use as replacing creativity and humanity, producing a strong negative reaction.
